Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Gold overtakes U.S. Treasuries as the largest foreign reserve asset 43 upvotes, 1 comments
  2. Go on Embedded Systems and WebAssembly 130 upvotes, 18 comments
  3. Fake Fans 81 upvotes, 13 comments
  4. Herbie: Automatically improve imprecise floating point formulas 46 upvotes, 6 comments
  5. Show HN: I built a frontpage for personal blogs 682 upvotes, 173 comments
  6. iNaturalist 362 upvotes, 100 comments
  7. The FAA’s flight restriction for drones is an attempt to criminalize filming ICE 267 upvotes, 72 comments
  8. Build your own Dial-up ISP with a Raspberry Pi 119 upvotes, 28 comments
  9. Europe asks if reviving nuclear is the answer to energy shocks 21 upvotes, 2 comments
  10. What changes when you turn a Linux box into a router 118 upvotes, 32 comments
  11. Show HN: Travel Hacking Toolkit – Points search and trip planning with AI 29 upvotes, 5 comments
  12. Artemis II crew take “spectacular” image of Earth 577 upvotes, 225 comments
  13. Delve Removed from Y Combinator 79 upvotes, 26 comments
  14. Run Linux containers on Android, no root required 48 upvotes, 14 comments
  15. We replaced RAG with a virtual filesystem for our AI documentation assistant 245 upvotes, 103 comments
  16. Post Mortem: axios NPM supply chain compromise 206 upvotes, 86 comments
  17. Show HN: TinyOS – A minimalist RTOS for Cortex-M written in C 91 upvotes, 33 comments
  18. Oracle Files H-1B Visa Petitions Amid Mass Layoffs 435 upvotes, 242 comments
  19. The house is a work of art: Frank Lloyd Wright 56 upvotes, 24 comments
  20. How to make a sliding, self-locking, and predator-proof chicken coop door (2020) 82 upvotes, 41 comments
  21. OpenClaw privilege escalation vulnerability 275 upvotes, 165 comments
  22. Bourbon waste could provide next-gen supercapacitor components 21 upvotes, 12 comments
  23. Tell HN: Anthropic no longer allowing Claude Code subscriptions to use OpenClaw 382 upvotes, 391 comments
  24. Windows++: C++ Application Framework for Windows by Paul DiLascia 10 upvotes, 4 comments
  25. The Technocracy Movement of the 1930s 71 upvotes, 69 comments
  26. Big-Endian Testing with QEMU 80 upvotes, 80 comments
  27. Improving my focus by giving up my big monitor 42 upvotes, 62 comments
  28. An experimental guide to Answer Engine Optimization 4 upvotes, 2 comments
  29. F-15E jet shot down over Iran 402 upvotes, 943 comments
  30. Charge Robotics (YC S21) Is Hiring Software and Hardware Engineers 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 12:00pm, 4 April 2026. Source here.
